title: ulmus minor var. lockii
intro: Ulmus minor var. lockii, commonly known as the Dutch Elm or Wych Elm, is a variety of the small-leaved elm tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its content of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Ulmus minor var. lockii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various conditions. Herbal preparations of Ulmus minor var. lockii can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other illnesses.
